translations.title: yesid.dev — Portfolio Site
translations.one_liner: The site you are looking at: a SvelteKit portfolio built slice by slice.
translations.description: A personal brand and portfolio site for a freelance SQL developer and digital infrastructure consultant. Built with SvelteKit 2, Svelte 5, Tailwind CSS v4, and deployed to Vercel. Designed to be multilingual (en/fr/es) from day one.

translations.title: Why SvelteKit?
translations.content: SvelteKit compiles away the framework at build time, producing lean HTML and minimal JavaScript. For a portfolio site where first impression and load speed matter, that trade-off is worth making.

translations.title: Transit Operations Data Pipeline
translations.one_liner: An end-to-end ELT pipeline processing real-time transit data for a regional operator.
translations.description: A production data pipeline ingesting GTFS-RT feeds, transforming them with dbt, and surfacing KPIs in a Power BI dashboard. Built for a transit authority in Quebec.

translations.title: Lorem Analytics Dashboard
translations.one_liner: Executive KPI dashboard tracking operational metrics across 12 departments.
translations.description: A Power BI dashboard suite built for a logistics company. Pulls data from SQL Server, applies business logic in DAX, and delivers daily refreshes to executive stakeholders. Reduced reporting time from 2 days to 15 minutes.

translations.title: The Problem
translations.content: Operations managers were spending two days each month compiling reports from multiple spreadsheets. Data was stale by the time decisions were made.

translations.title: The Approach
translations.content: Connected directly to SQL Server with scheduled refreshes. Built a semantic layer in DAX so business users could slice data without writing queries.

translations.title: Lorem Database Migration
translations.one_liner: Zero-downtime migration from legacy MySQL to PostgreSQL for a SaaS platform.
translations.description: Migrated a 500GB MySQL database to PostgreSQL with zero downtime using dual-write and shadow reads. Included schema redesign, data type mapping, and stored procedure conversion.

translations.title: Why Migrate?
translations.content: The legacy MySQL instance was hitting performance limits. PostgreSQL offered better JSON support, CTEs, and window functions needed for the analytics layer.

translations.title: Migration Strategy
translations.content: Used a dual-write pattern: new writes go to both databases, reads gradually shift to PostgreSQL. Shadow reads validated correctness before the cutover.

translations.title: Lorem Query Optimizer
translations.one_liner: Automated SQL Server query analysis tool that identifies slow queries and suggests index improvements.
translations.description: A Python-based tool that connects to SQL Server, analyzes execution plans, identifies missing indexes, and generates optimization recommendations. Reduced average query time by 73% across 200+ stored procedures.

translations.title: How It Works
translations.content: Connects to the target SQL Server instance, captures execution plans for the heaviest queries, and analyzes them for common anti-patterns: table scans, implicit conversions, and parameter sniffing issues.

translations.title: Lorem Retool Admin Panel
translations.one_liner: Internal operations dashboard for managing inventory and approval workflows.
translations.description: A Retool-based admin panel that replaced 6 spreadsheets with a unified interface. CRUD operations on PostgreSQL, role-based access control, and automated approval routing.

translations.title: Before & After
translations.content: Operations ran on 6 Google Sheets with manual copy-paste between them. The Retool panel centralized everything into one interface with real-time PostgreSQL queries.
